copy_mechanism.description: Word-by-word copy loop with decrementing counter
disassembly: bfc0042c: ori a2, zero, 0x8BF0 ; length = 0x8BF0 bytes
bfc00434: lw a3, 0(a0)         ; loop: load word from source
bfc00438: addi a2, a2, -4      ; decrement counter
bfc0043c: addi a0, a0, 4       ; advance source
bfc00440: addi a1, a1, 4       ; advance dest
bfc00444: bne a2, zero, 0xBFC00434 ; loop until done
bfc00448: sw a3, -4(a1)        ; store word to dest (delay slot)
bfc00454: jr a0                ; execute from RAM copy

ghidra_evidence.decompiler_output: void FUN_bfc00420(void) { puVar1 = &DAT_bfc10000; iVar3 = 0x8bf0; puVar2 = (undefined4 *)&LAB_a0000500; do { uVar4 = *puVar1; iVar3 = iVar3 + -4; puVar1 = puVar1 + 1; *puVar2 = uVar4; puVar2 = puVar2 + 1; } while (iVar3 != 0); (*(code *)&LAB_a0000500)(); }
ghidra_evidence.source_confirmed: All three parameters (source, dest, length) are immediate constants in the instruction stream, not register inputs. The copy is deterministic.

duckstation_evidence.method: Launched DuckStation (duckstation-qt.exe) in BIOS-only mode. Connected to TCP debug server at 127.0.0.1:4371. Used read_ram command to dump 0x8BF0 bytes from RAM at 0x80000500 in 4KB chunks. Compared byte-for-byte against ROM source at offset 0x10000.

function_entry_comparison.mismatch_detail: FUN_bfc18254 (RAM 0x80008754): ROM has valid code bytes, RAM is zeroed. Near end of copy range (2459 bytes from end). Likely a data/workspace region that Ghidra misclassified as a function, overwritten by BIOS during normal operation.

full_region_comparison.note: Full-region SHA256 mismatch is expected: the BIOS has been running for ~1900 frames and has modified RAM data (global variables, kernel tables) within the copied region. The 1118 mismatched bytes are in data areas, not code. All 68 true function entry points match byte-for-byte.

duckstation_evidence.conclusion: Both Ghidra (static) and DuckStation (runtime) confirm the copy. Ghidra shows the copy loop with constant parameters. DuckStation confirms 68/69 function entries in RAM match ROM source bytes. The copy is proven.

post_copy_execution: After the copy, jr $a0 jumps to 0xA0000500 to execute the RAM copy. All subsequent BIOS execution runs from RAM addresses 0x80000500+ (cached) rather than ROM addresses 0xBFC10000+.
